title: Simulate Orbital Mechanics
description: Write a numerical simulation of planetary orbits using Newton's law of gravitation and a Runge-Kutta integrator. Reproduce Kepler's laws from first principles.
whyItMatters: Orbital mechanics is where classical mechanics meets computational physics. Simulating orbits teaches numerical methods, conservation laws, and the beauty of emergent behaviour (elliptical orbits) arising from a simple force law (1/r²).
skillsLearned: Conservation of energy and angular momentum

steps.title: Set up the physics
steps.description: Write the gravitational force between two bodies: F = -GMm/r² in vector form. Express as a system of ODEs: dx/dt = v, dv/dt = F/m.
steps.tips: Work in dimensionless units (set G*M = 1) to avoid numerical overflow.

steps.title: Implement Euler method
steps.description: Code a simple Euler integrator and simulate a circular orbit. Observe energy drift over time.
steps.tips: Euler's method is inaccurate for orbits — energy drifts. This motivates better methods.

steps.title: Upgrade to RK4
steps.description: Implement a fourth-order Runge-Kutta integrator. Compare energy conservation with the Euler method.
steps.tips: RK4 conserves energy much better. Plot total energy vs time to verify.

steps.title: Reproduce Kepler's laws
steps.description: Launch a planet with different initial velocities to get circular, elliptical, parabolic, and hyperbolic orbits. Verify Kepler's equal-areas law and the period-radius relationship (T² proportional to a³).
steps.tips: Use matplotlib to animate the orbit in real time.

steps.title: Extend to three bodies
steps.description: Add a third body and observe chaotic behaviour. Try the figure-8 three-body orbit with specific initial conditions.
steps.tips: The three-body problem is chaotic — tiny changes in initial conditions lead to wildly different trajectories.

deliverables: Working Python simulation of two-body orbits
Plots showing circular, elliptical, parabolic, and hyperbolic trajectories
Energy conservation comparison between Euler and RK4

rubric.description: Gravitational force implemented correctly, orbits match expected shapes
RK4 implemented correctly, energy conserved to high precision
All three laws demonstrated with quantitative evidence
Clean, commented code with modular functions
